author: potuz
content: Thanks, will look when I wake up. Will need to figure out why we go into init sync to start with.

author: potuz
content: did you guys restart the execution node? I'd like to get the logs around epoch 980 to see what caused the split

author: edgl_dev
content: not that i know, maybe a watchtower push made it restart

author: potuz
content: ok, anyway I did find a stupid lack of optimization on our part: We submit a block and our peers start immediately sending us our own data column sidecars and we ignore them cause we haven't imported our own block yet

author: potuz
content: and we then fail to build the next block correctly

author: potuz
content:
This shouldn't break the chain though and it seems that ethrex fails to construct the next block in 31388, we get this message 
``` 
[2026-06-29 20:07:48.39] DEBUG rpc/validator: Received execution payload from local engine value=&{neg:false abs:[195389954000000000]}
```

author: potuz
content: Yeah <@1443132403001589771> I'm pretty sure ethrex had a problem to sync the payload envelope for 31388 which would have caused a reorg on ethrex

author: potuz
content: and that breaks Prysm there, we had a bug that made us attempt to reorg that payload (because we never synced it) but ethrex never recovers from that

author: potuz
content: I'll fix our issue, but the stall seems to come from ethrex not recovering from that slot

author: potuz
content: We declare that payload unavailable cause we can't get the columns and then we're stuck forever

author: potuz
content: I'll restart that node cause it'll catch up to head now, that I have the diagnosis, but you guys should look into what happened at that slot, will fix our broadcasting issue as well

author: edgl_dev
content:
just installed the panda cli to check

 ethrex logs for that window show that it didn't fail to build or reorg at 31388 ( 2026-06-29):
20:07:31  FCU V4 (payload attrs) -> building slot=31388
20:07:43  BLOCK 22927 0x0e5fb318… executed, valid          (canonical 31388)
20:07:48  FCU V4 (payload attrs) -> building slot=31389
20:07:48  BLOCK 22927 0x76eccfbc… executed, valid          (the 31389 that got orphaned)
20:18:51  FCU V4 (payload attrs) -> building slot=31445     (resumes as soon as the CL calls again)
It built payloads for both 31388 and 31389, executed both fork candidates without an error, and there are zero error/warn/reorg lines on the EL from 20:00 (06-29) until a watchtower image update restarted it at 12:02 (06-30). The EL only goes idle in the spans where it receives no FCU/newPayload from the beacon
i can't see anything odd from the logs

author: potuz
content: That's very weird, we never get to sync the payload for 31388, but in fact it's not your payload, we ditched your payload and took Buildoors, so it wasn't our columns that we didn't sync, it was buildoors

author: potuz
content: may be the same thing as always, we get data columns before the block and we do not sync them

author: potuz
content: What do other clients do here? do you guys put the columns in a pending queue? isn't this a brutal DOS?
